sections.paragraph:
σαρκίον, ου, τό dim. of σάρξ a part or piece of a body, piece of flesh, bit of flesh (Hippocr. et al.; M. Ant. 2, 2, 1; 2) of an entire body (‘bit of flesh’ Plut., Brut. 987 [8, 3]) κοινωνῆσαι τῷ ἁγίῳ(κοινωνέω 1bα) to share some moments with his holy bit of fleshMPol 17:1.—DELG s.v. σάρξ.

dim. dim. = diminutive

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 914
